info.title: DeploymentsClient
info.description: Provides operations for working with deployments.

get.description: Get all the deployments at the given scope.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: The fully qualified Azure Resource manager identifier of the resource.

parameters.name: $filter
parameters.description: The filter to apply on the operation. For example, you can use $filter=provisioningState eq '{state}'.

parameters.name: $top
parameters.description: The number of results to get. If null is passed, returns all deployments.

get.description: Gets a deployment.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: The fully qualified Azure Resource manager identifier of the resource.

put.summary: Deploys resources at a given scope.
put.description: You can provide the template and parameters directly in the request or link to JSON files.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: The fully qualified Azure Resource manager identifier of the resource.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: Additional parameters supplied to the operation.

Location.description: The Location header contains the URL where the status of the long running operation can be checked.

Retry-After.description: The Retry-After header can indicate how long the client should wait before polling the operation status.

delete.summary: Deletes a deployment from the deployment history.
delete.description: A template deployment that is currently running cannot be deleted. Deleting a template deployment removes the associated deployment operations. This is an asynchronous operation that returns a status of 202 until the template deployment is successfully deleted. The Location response header contains the URI that is used to obtain the status of the process. While the process is running, a call to the URI in the Location header returns a status of 202. When the process finishes, the URI in the Location header returns a status of 204 on success. If the asynchronous request failed, the URI in the Location header returns an error-level status code.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: The fully qualified Azure Resource manager identifier of the resource.

Location.description: The Location header contains the URL where the status of the long running operation can be checked.

Retry-After.description: The Retry-After header can indicate how long the client should wait before polling the operation status.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: The fully qualified Azure Resource manager identifier of the resource.

404.description: The Azure resource is not found

post.summary: Cancels a currently running template deployment.
post.description: You can cancel a deployment only if the provisioningState is Accepted or Running. After the deployment is canceled, the provisioningState is set to Canceled. Canceling a template deployment stops the currently running template deployment and leaves the resources partially deployed.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: The fully qualified Azure Resource manager identifier of the resource.

delete.summary: Deletes a deployment from the deployment history.
delete.description: A template deployment that is currently running cannot be deleted. Deleting a template deployment removes the associated deployment operations. Deleting a template deployment does not affect the state of the resource group. This is an asynchronous operation that returns a status of 202 until the template deployment is successfully deleted. The Location response header contains the URI that is used to obtain the status of the process. While the process is running, a call to the URI in the Location header returns a status of 202. When the process finishes, the URI in the Location header returns a status of 204 on success. If the asynchronous request failed, the URI in the Location header returns an error-level status code.

parameters.name: resourceGroupName
parameters.description: The name of the resource group.

Location.description: The Location header contains the URL where the status of the long running operation can be checked.

Retry-After.description: The Retry-After header can indicate how long the client should wait before polling the operation status.

parameters.name: resourceGroupName
parameters.description: The name of the resource group.

404.description: The Azure resource is not found

post.summary: Cancels a currently running template deployment.
post.description: You can cancel a deployment only if the provisioningState is Accepted or Running. After the deployment is canceled, the provisioningState is set to Canceled. Canceling a template deployment stops the currently running template deployment and leaves the resource group partially deployed.

parameters.name: resourceGroupName
parameters.description: The name of the resource group.

defaultPath.description: The default path for an alias.

defaultPattern.description: The default pattern for an alias.

defaultMetadata.description: The default alias path metadata. Applies to the default path and to any alias path that doesn't have metadata

AliasPath.description: The type of the paths for alias.

pattern.description: The pattern for an alias path.

metadata.description: The metadata of the alias path. If missing, fall back to the default metadata of the alias.

AliasPathAttributes.description: The attributes of the token that the alias path is referring to.

values.name: None
values.description: The token that the alias path is referring to has no attributes.

values.name: Modifiable
values.description: The token that the alias path is referring to is modifiable by policies with 'modify' effect.

type.description: The type of the token that the alias path is referring to.

attributes.description: The attributes of the token that the alias path is referring to.

AliasPathTokenType.description: The type of the token that the alias path is referring to.

values.name: NotSpecified
values.description: The token type is not specified.

values.name: Any
values.description: The token type can be anything.

AliasPattern.description: The type of the pattern for an alias path.

values.name: Extract
values.description: Extract is the only allowed value.

values.name: NotSpecified
values.description: Alias type is unknown (same as not providing alias type).

ChangeType.description: Type of change that will be made to the resource when the deployment is executed.

values.name: Create
values.description: The resource does not exist in the current state but is present in the desired state. The resource will be created when the deployment is executed.

values.name: Delete
values.description: The resource exists in the current state and is missing from the desired state. The resource will be deleted when the deployment is executed.

values.name: Ignore
values.description: The resource exists in the current state and is missing from the desired state. The resource will not be deployed or modified when the deployment is executed.

values.name: Deploy
values.description: The resource exists in the current state and the desired state and will be redeployed when the deployment is executed. The properties of the resource may or may not change.

values.name: NoChange
values.description: The resource exists in the current state and the desired state and will be redeployed when the deployment is executed. The properties of the resource will not change.

values.name: Modify
values.description: The resource exists in the current state and the desired state and will be redeployed when the deployment is executed. The properties of the resource will change.

values.name: Unsupported
values.description: The resource is not supported by What-If.

CloudError.description: An error response for a resource management request.

error.description: Common error response for all Azure Resource Manager APIs to return error details for failed operations. (This also follows the OData error response format.)

detailLevel.description: Specifies the type of information to log for debugging. The permitted values are none, requestContent, responseContent, or both requestContent and responseContent separated by a comma. The default is none. When setting this value, carefully consider the type of information you are passing in during deployment. By logging information about the request or response, you could potentially expose sensitive data that is retrieved through the deployment operations.

location.description: The location to store the deployment data.

identity.description: The Managed Identity configuration for a deployment.

type.description: The value type of the extension config property.

value.description: The value of the extension config property.

keyVaultReference.description: The Azure Key Vault reference used to retrieve the secret value of the extension config property.

alias.description: The alias of the extension as defined in the deployment template.

configId.description: The extension configuration ID. It uniquely identifies a deployment control plane within an extension.

DeploymentExternalInputDefinition.description: Deployment external input definition for parameterization.

DeploymentIdentity.description: The Managed Identity configuration for a deployment.

userAssignedIdentities.description: The set of user assigned identities associated with the resource.

value.description: The DeploymentExtended items on this page

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

DeploymentMode.description: The mode that is used to deploy resources. This value can be either Incremental or Complete. In Incremental mode, resources are deployed without deleting existing resources that are not included in the template. In Complete mode, resources are deployed and existing resources in the resource group that are not included in the template are deleted. Be careful when using Complete mode as you may unintentionally delete resources.

provisioningOperation.description: The name of the current provisioning operation.

timestamp.description: The date and time of the operation.

statusCode.description: Operation status code from the resource provider. This property may not be set if a response has not yet been received.

statusMessage.description: Operation status message from the resource provider. This property is optional.  It will only be provided if an error was received from the resource provider.

value.description: The DeploymentOperation items on this page

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

template.description: The template content. You use this element when you want to pass the template syntax directly in the request rather than link to an existing template. It can be a JObject or well-formed JSON string. Use either the templateLink property or the template property, but not both.

templateLink.description: The URI of the template. Use either the templateLink property or the template property, but not both.

parameters.description: Name and value pairs that define the deployment parameters for the template. You use this element when you want to provide the parameter values directly in the request rather than link to an existing parameter file. Use either the parametersLink property or the parameters property, but not both. It can be a JObject or a well formed JSON string.

externalInputs.description: External input values, used by external tooling for parameter evaluation.

externalInputDefinitions.description: External input definitions, used by external tooling to define expected external input values.

parametersLink.description: The URI of parameters file. You use this element to link to an existing parameters file. Use either the parametersLink property or the parameters property, but not both.

extensionConfigs.description: The configurations to use for deployment extensions. The keys of this object are deployment extension aliases as defined in the deployment template.

mode.description: The mode that is used to deploy resources. This value can be either Incremental or Complete. In Incremental mode, resources are deployed without deleting existing resources that are not included in the template. In Complete mode, resources are deployed and existing resources in the resource group that are not included in the template are deleted. Be careful when using Complete mode as you may unintentionally delete resources.

debugSetting.description: The debug setting of the deployment.

expressionEvaluationOptions.description: Specifies whether template expressions are evaluated within the scope of the parent template or nested template. Only applicable to nested templates. If not specified, default value is outer.

validationLevel.description: The validation level of the deployment

correlationId.description: The correlation ID of the deployment.

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the template deployment.

duration.description: The duration of the template deployment.

outputs.description: Key/value pairs that represent deployment output.

providers.description: The list of resource providers needed for the deployment.

extensions.description: The extensions used in this deployment.

mode.description: The deployment mode. Possible values are Incremental and Complete.

debugSetting.description: The debug setting of the deployment.

templateHash.description: The hash produced for the template.

diagnostics.description: Contains diagnostic information collected during validation process.

validationLevel.description: The validation level of the deployment

DeploymentValidateResult.description: Information from validate template deployment response.

location.description: The location to store the deployment data.

resultFormat.description: The format of the What-If results

ExpressionEvaluationOptions.description: Specifies whether template expressions are evaluated within the scope of the parent template or nested template.

scope.description: The scope to be used for evaluation of parameters, variables and functions in a nested template.

ExpressionEvaluationOptionsScopeType.description: The scope to be used for evaluation of parameters, variables and functions in a nested template.

values.name: String
values.description: Property type representing a string value.

values.name: Int
values.description: Property type representing an integer value.

values.name: Bool
values.description: Property type representing a boolean value.

values.name: Array
values.description: Property type representing an array value.

values.name: Object
values.description: Property type representing an object value.

values.name: SecureString
values.description: Property type representing a secure string value.

values.name: SecureObject
values.description: Property type representing a secure object value.

type.description: The deployment on error behavior type. Possible values are LastSuccessful and SpecificDeployment.

deploymentName.description: The deployment to be used on error case.

OnErrorDeploymentExtended.description: Deployment on error behavior with additional details.

provisioningState.description: The state of the provisioning for the on error deployment.

type.description: The deployment on error behavior type. Possible values are LastSuccessful and SpecificDeployment.

deploymentName.description: The deployment to be used on error case.

OnErrorDeploymentType.description: The deployment on error behavior type. Possible values are LastSuccessful and SpecificDeployment.

ParametersLink.description: Entity representing the reference to the deployment parameters.

uri.description: The URI of the parameters file.

contentVersion.description: If included, must match the ContentVersion in the template.

values.name: Create
values.description: The property does not exist in the current state but is present in the desired state. The property will be created when the deployment is executed.

values.name: Delete
values.description: The property exists in the current state and is missing from the desired state. It will be deleted when the deployment is executed.

values.name: Modify
values.description: The property exists in both current and desired state and is different. The value of the property will change when the deployment is executed.

values.name: Array
values.description: The property is an array and contains nested changes.

values.name: NoEffect
values.description: The property will not be set or updated.

namespace.description: The namespace of the resource provider.

registrationState.description: The registration state of the resource provider.

registrationPolicy.description: The registration policy of the resource provider.

resourceTypes.description: The collection of provider resource types.

extendedLocations.description: The extended locations for the azure location.

ProviderResourceType.description: Resource type managed by the resource provider.

locations.description: The collection of locations where this resource type can be created.

locationMappings.description: The location mappings that are supported by this resource type.

aliases.description: The aliases that are supported by this resource type.

apiProfiles.description: The API profiles for the resource provider.

capabilities.description: The additional capabilities offered by this resource type.

ProvisioningOperation.description: The name of the current provisioning operation.

values.name: NotSpecified
values.description: The provisioning operation is not specified.

values.name: AzureAsyncOperationWaiting
values.description: The provisioning operation is waiting Azure async operation.

values.name: ResourceCacheWaiting
values.description: The provisioning operation is waiting for resource cache.

values.name: EvaluateDeploymentOutput
values.description: The provisioning operation is evaluate output.

values.name: DeploymentCleanup
values.description: The provisioning operation is cleanup. This operation is part of the 'complete' mode deployment.

id.description: The fully qualified Azure resource ID.

extension.description: The extension the resource was deployed with.

apiVersion.description: The API version the resource was deployed with.

location.description: The location to store the deployment data.
The location to store the deployment data.

error.description: The error reported by the operation.

id.description: The Azure resource ID of the resource.

extension.description: The extension the resource was deployed with.

apiVersion.description: The API version the resource was deployed with.

symbolicName.description: The symbolic name of the resource as defined in the deployment template.

TemplateHashResult.description: Result of the request to calculate template hash. It contains a string of minified template and its hash.

TemplateLink.description: Entity representing the reference to the template.

uri.description: The URI of the template to deploy. Use either the uri or id property, but not both.

id.description: The resource id of a Template Spec. Use either the id or uri property, but not both.

relativePath.description: The relativePath property can be used to deploy a linked template at a location relative to the parent. If the parent template was linked with a TemplateSpec, this will reference an artifact in the TemplateSpec.  If the parent was linked with a URI, the child deployment will be a combination of the parent and relativePath URIs

contentVersion.description: If included, must match the ContentVersion in the template.

queryString.description: The query string (for example, a SAS token) to be used with the templateLink URI.

ValidationLevel.description: The level of validation performed on the deployment.

values.name: Template
values.description: Static analysis of the template is performed.

values.name: Provider
values.description: Static analysis of the template is performed and resource declarations are sent to resource providers for semantic validation. Validates that the caller has RBAC write permissions on each resource.

values.name: ProviderNoRbac
values.description: Static analysis of the template is performed and resource declarations are sent to resource providers for semantic validation. Skips validating that the caller has RBAC write permissions on each resource.

WhatIfChange.description: Information about a single resource change predicted by What-If operation.

deploymentId.description: The resource id of the Deployment responsible for this change.

symbolicName.description: The symbolic name of the resource responsible for this change.

identifiers.description: A subset of properties that uniquely identify a Bicep extensible resource because it lacks a resource id like an Azure resource has.

extension.description: The extension the resource was deployed with.

changeType.description: Type of change that will be made to the resource when the deployment is executed.

unsupportedReason.description: The explanation about why the resource is unsupported by What-If.

before.description: The snapshot of the resource before the deployment is executed.

after.description: The predicted snapshot of the resource after the deployment is executed.

delta.description: The predicted changes to resource properties.

changes.description: List of resource changes predicted by What-If operation.

potentialChanges.description: List of resource changes predicted by What-If operation.

diagnostics.description: List of resource diagnostics detected by What-If operation.

WhatIfOperationResult.description: Result of the What-If operation. Contains a list of predicted changes and a URL link to get to the next set of results.

WhatIfPropertyChange.description: The predicted change to the resource property.

before.description: The value of the property before the deployment is executed.

after.description: The value of the property after the deployment is executed.

WhatIfResultFormat.description: The format of the What-If results

location.description: The location of the zone mapping.
